description_long: This is a classic stovetop moka pot coffee maker, featuring an octagonal upper chamber and a matching lower water reservoir, both with a matte black finish. The body is constructed from metal, likely aluminum or stainless steel, with visible wear and patina on the lower section, suggesting frequent use. The lid is flat and faceted, topped with a small, black, polygonal knob for easy opening. A robust, angular black handle is attached to the side, made from heat-resistant plastic, providing a secure grip. The spout is short and integrated into the upper chamber, designed for pouring brewed coffee. No visible branding, control panels, or electrical components are present, indicating this is a manual, stovetop appliance.
description: A matte black, octagonal stovetop moka pot with a polygonal knob, sturdy black handle, and visible wear on the lower chamber. The design is classic and functional, with no visible branding or electronic controls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the octagonal lid, central knob, and spout, with the handle not visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angular black handle and spout, with the lid and knob clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full handle attachment and the back of the pot, with the spout hidden.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle from a different angle and the spout partially visible.
